                                                      MEB-TUS 
                                                      WILMINGTON, POW
                                                      171700JMAR25
                                                          
FRAGMENTARY ORDER (FRAGO) 002 TO OPORD POTOMAC GUARD 

(U) References: No Changes from Basic Order.
(U) Time Zone Used throughout OpOrd: J

1. (U) Situation. MEB-TUS is expected to transition to Phase II, Stage A within the next 72 hrs. In preparation for this transition MEB-TUS has requested CLR-27 initiate planning to establish a forward LSA IVO New Bern, TUS ISO MEB-TUS operations. 

2. (U) Mission. On order CLR-27 will establish Role 2 a forward Logistical Support Area (LSA) IVO New Bern, TUS IOT support MEB-TUS scheme of maneuver.    

3. (U) Execution 

    a. (U) Commander’s Intent

        (1) (U) Purpose.  Provide forward logistical support to RCT-2 IOT support the ground scheme of maneuver and provide sustainment IVO New Bern, TUS.

        (2) (U) Method.  CLR-27 will establish a forward LSA utilizing a phased approach to equip the forward LSA with logistical capabilities in accordance with the most immediate needs of RCT-2. CLR-27 will utilize organic transportation assets to the extent possible.

        (3) (U) Endstate.  Establish forward logistics combat element capable of providing timely sustainment and maintenance support ISO MEB-TUS forces; enemy unable to disrupt forward logistical activities.

    b. (U) Concept of Operations. No Change. 

    c. (U) Concept of Maneuver. No Change.  

    d. (U) Tasks

        (1) (U) CLR-27

            (a) (U) Conduct mission analysis IOT identify most immediate logistical requirements ISO RCT-2 IOT facilitate a phased establishment of LSA.

            (b) (U) Identify key terrain IVO New Bern, TUS IOT establish forward LSA at a location that will facilitate logistical operations in a defensible position.

            (c) (U) Provide continuous operational level logistics support IOT sustain MEB-TUS operations. 

            (d) (U) Maintain Movement Control Center IOT facilitate tracking and synchronization of movements during the establishment of the LSA.

            (e) (U) Establish and maintain total asset visibility while in transit, to include commodities, equipment, personnel, units, and ground assets IOT ensure accountability and accurate reporting. 

            (f) (U) BPT to conduct functions of logistics from LSA.

            (g) (U) BPT maintain 5 DOS of Class I/III/V supplies on hand at LSA.

            (h) (U) BPT provide security during all phases of establishing LSA. 

            (i) (U) BPT support DS logistics for RCT-2 and provide GS logistics to MEB-TUS from LSA IOT provide timely support to offensive operations. 

            (j) (U) Submit ConOps with recommended date and time of earliest execution for approval. Upon review, MEB-TUS will issue an Execute Order (ExOrd) with an approved timeline for execution. 

            (k) (U) Transmit all items of intelligence interest to MEB-TUS G-2 during the conduct of the mission. 

        (2) (U) MAG-26 

            (a) (U) BPT provide ALS ISO CLR-27 in establishment of LSA IVO New Bern, TUS.

            (b) (U) BPT provide armed reconnaissance during the establishment of the LSA.

            (c) (U) BPT provide on-call close air support if needed IOT defend LSA.  

    e. (U) Coordinating Instructions.

        (1) (U) Commander’s Critical Information Requirements.  No change.

        (2) (U) Information Operations. Submit all IO products and broadcasts to MEB-TUS for routing and approval prior to distribution.  
    
4. (U) Administration and Logistics
  
    a.  Admin:  No change.

    b.  Logistics: Identify all equipment shortfalls to MEB-TUS G-4 NLT 221500JMAR25.

5. (U) Command and Signal
             
    a.  Command: Command Relationships:  No change.

    b.  Signal: No Change.

ACKNOWLEDGE RECEIPT



                                   s/
                                   GARY. C. THOMAS
                                   Lt Col, USMC
